title: Test fixtures
description: Add a minimal_xlsx pytest fixture to tests/conftest.py (or a new tests/conftest_xlsx.py imported from conftest.py). The fixture creates a workbook programmatically via openpyxl with at least two sheets; the first sheet has a header row and at least three data rows containing int, float, str, bool, and datetime values. Returns the Path to the file in tmp_path.
acceptance_criteria: minimal_xlsx fixture is accessible to all test_xlsx_*.py files
Fixture workbook contains at least two sheets
First sheet has a header row and at least 3 data rows with mixed types including int, float, str, bool, and datetime
No binary .xlsx files are committed — workbook is created programmatically at test time

handoff_summary.completed: minimal_xlsx fixture added to tests/conftest.py with two sheets, mixed-type data rows, and no committed binary files. openpyxl added to dev dependencies.
handoff_summary.remaining: No remaining work for this bead.
handoff_summary.risks: openpyxl is now a dev dependency; downstream test beads must run via uv run pytest to pick it up automatically.
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to the tester agent to verify the fixture resolves correctly across all test_xlsx_*.py files.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Sibling beads that also modify pyproject.toml dev deps may conflict on that block. The conftest.py change is additive and should not conflict with existing pptx fixtures.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: openpyxl import is kept inside the fixture body (lazy import) to avoid a hard import-time failure when openpyxl is absent, matching the pattern for optional-dependency fixtures.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The fixture itself is the test infrastructure; coverage is exercised when test_xlsx_*.py files use it.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: openpyxl stores Python bool values as booleans in OOXML; behaviour is correct but downstream tests should be aware that True/False may round-trip differently depending on openpyxl version.

conflict_risks: Sibling beads that also modify pyproject.toml dev deps may conflict on that block. The conftest.py change is additive and should not conflict with existing pptx fixtures.

execution_history.summary: Added minimal_xlsx pytest fixture to tests/conftest.py using openpyxl. The fixture creates a two-sheet workbook ("Data" + "Summary") programmatically in tmp_path. Sheet "Data" has a header row and 3 data rows with int, float, str, bool, and datetime values. Also added openpyxl>=3.1 to the dev dependency group in pyproject.toml so the fixture works under uv run pytest.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
